rulings.Case Title: ALIPHCOM, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. ELIZABETH BASTIAANSE HAMREN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Plantronics To Produce Docs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 21, 2010, line 1. PLAINTIFF ALIPHCOM'S Motion To Compel Plantronics To Produce Docs - duplicate entry. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ALIPHCOM, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. ELIZABETH BASTIAANSE HAMREN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plantronics To Produce Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 21, 2010, line 1. PLAINTIFF ALIPHCOM'S (Amended) Notice Of Motion To Compel Plantronics To Produce Documents is denied. Plaintiff's requests are overbroad and burdensome. (See Calcor Space Facility Inc. v. Superior Court of Orange County (1997) 53 Cal.App. 4th 216). Aliphcom may review the documents that Plantronics has provided, then propound more narrowly tailored and calculated requests if necessary. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CONCORDE EQUITY 11, LLC VS. Q-BLACK, et al

PLAINTIFF CONCORDE EQUITY 11, L.L.C.'S Motion To Have Defendants Held In Contempt For Failing To Comply With Turnover Order is continued to September 29, 2010. Moving party to provide courtesy copies of its papers to Department 302 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JEROME J. GHIGLIOTTI JR. VS. LEO KAISER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Discovery 612, Defendant Leo Kaiser'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Issue Sanctions, To Compel Discovery (Continued From 9/14/10)
rulings.Rulings: Discovery 612, Defendant Leo Kaiser's Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Issue Sanctions, To Compel Discovery is denied. Commissioner Hewlett's order was filed August 3, 2010 and ordered cross-defendant to respond to discovery within 15 days of notice of the order and pay sanctions within 30 days of notice of the order. Cross-defendant's actions were timely. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD WAXMAN et al VS. ENTERTAINMENTXPRESS, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notcie Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgemtn Pursuant To Ccp Sec 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 21, 2010, line 10. PLAINTIFFS RICHARD WAXMAN, IMRAN SAYEED'S Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To C.C.P. Sec 664.6. Granted in part, no opposition filed. Judgment entered as to missed payments under settlement agreement. No award of attorney's fees and costs because not provided for in settlement agreement. Attorneys' fees provisions ambiguous as to whether they are only awarded upon an adjudication of a claim for a withholding. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: EDDIE CHEUNG VS. FRANK CHEUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Strike Deft'S Memorandum Of Costs In Its Entirety
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 21, 2010, line 6. PLAINTIFF EDDIE CHEUNG'S Motion To Strike Defendant's Memorandum Of Costs In Its Entirety. Motion granted in part. Defendant is the prevailing party for purposes of this motion. Plaintiff properly objects to attorney fees that have no basis in contract or statute. Plaintiff properly objects to the "other" category and no showing by defendant that any of those costs are authorized by code, are reasonable, or are necessary. Costs are taxed and reduced by $7,996.48. Motion denied in all other respects. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ONEWEST BANK, FSB VS. ESTEBAN POLONSKI et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 21, 2010, line 7. PLAINTIFF ONEWEST BANK, F.S.B.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Burden shifted and defendant fails to raise a triable issue of fact. Defendant has not shown the sale was "void" as opposed to "voidable," and defendant has failed to tender. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH WALLACE VS. JERREMI CLARK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 21, 2010, line 8. DEFENDANT VIRGANEYCE CLARK'S Motion To Set Aside Default is granted, no opposition filed. Answer must be filed by September 24, 2010. Defendant to prepare order. =(302/CWW)
Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 21, 2010, line 9. DEFENDANT ROBERT TEMPLE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is off calendar. Dismissal without prejudice filed on August 31, 2010. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANNING JIANG VS. PATRICIA MULQUEEN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 21, 2010, line 11. DEFENDANT PATRICIA MULQUEEN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled, 5 days leave to answer. Whether the check was negotiable for $2,550.50 and complied with the S.F.A.C. cannot be determined on demurrer. The request for forfeiture is superfluous and does not render the notice defective. While the prayer for damages is improper, defendant should have brought a motion to strike. =(302/CWW)
